result.stdout:
LiterateCS 2.0.46
Copyright © 2022 Tommi Johtela
USAGE:
Generate markdown documentation for a solution:
  literatecs -t -d .md -e .cs -m DefaultTheme.dll -o docs -s <solution>.sln
  **.cs **.md
Create HTML documentation from files in a directory:
  literatecs -tv -d .md -e .cs -f html -i <root> -m DefaultTheme.dll -o docs
  src\*.cs *.md
Create HTML documentation for a solution:
  literatecs -tuv -d .md -e .cs -f html -m DefaultTheme.dll -o docs -s
  <solution>.sln src\*.cs *.md
Same example with long option names:
  literatecs --mdext .md --csext .cs --format html --theme DefaultTheme.dll
  --output docs --solution <solution>.sln --trim --updatetoc --verbose src\*.cs
  *.md

  -s, --solution        Read the the C# and markdown files from a msbuild
                        solution (*.sln). If this option is specified, the
                        '--input' option is ignored.

  -i, --input           The root folder for the files to be processed. Files
                        under this directory are checked against the filters. If
                        input folder is not specified, the current directory is
                        used.

  -o, --output          Output folder where the documentation will be generated
                        to. The default output folder is
                        <input|solutionfolder>/docs.

  -e, --csext           File extension for the C# source files. Used to identify
                        source files in the input folder. The default is '.cs'

  -d, --mdext           File extension for the markdown files. Used to identify
                        markdown files in the input folder. The default is '.md'

  -f, --format          Format of the outputted documentation; either 'md' or
                        'html'. Default is 'md'

  -t, --trim            Left-trim the comments before processing them to avoid
                        interpreting them as code blocks.

  -u, --updatetoc       Adds processed files that are not already in the TOC
                        file to it.

  -m, --theme           The theme DLL which generates HTML documents according
                        to page templates. If not specified, the default theme
                        is used.

  -b, --buildlog        Path to the build log file.

  -v, --verbose         Outputs information about processed files to the
                        standard output.

  --help                Display this help screen.

  --version             Display version information.

  <filters> (pos. 0)    Filters for input files; both source and markdown.

options.description: Path to the build log file.

Format of the outputted documentation; either 'md' or 'html'. Default is 'md'
The root folder for the files to be processed. Files under this directory are checked against the filters. If input folder is not specified, the current directory is used.
File extension for the markdown files. Used to identify markdown files in the input folder. The default is '.md'
Output folder where the documentation will be generated to. The default output folder is <input|solutionfolder>/docs.
Read the the C# and markdown files from a msbuild solution (*.sln). If this option is specified, the '--input' option is ignored.
